entry.title: Problem with Flexibility for Key Users Service in CF
entry.content:
Hello,

I have deployed my App in CF and I want to save Variants with  UI5 Flexibility for Key Users service.

 

Problem:

 

When I try to save a new Variant in VariantManagement I get an Error 500:

Payload:

What I have done:

I have Entitlement for UI5 Flexibility for Key Users service in my Subaccount.

Bootstrapping in my App:

 

data-sap-ui-flexibilityServices='[{"connector":"BtpServiceConnector","url":"/keyuser"}]'

 

Manifest.js:

 

"sap.ui5": { "flexEnabled": true,

 

My Standalone App router has this route:

 

{ "source": "^/keyuser/(.*)$", "target": "$1", "service": "com.sap.ui.flex.cf.keyuser", "endpoint": "keyuser" },

 

mta.yaml:

Creation of service instance for the UI5 flexibility:

 

- name: demoappdemoapp-ui5-flexibility-keyuser parameters: service: ui5-flexibility-keyuser service-plan: keyuser type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service

 

Binding of UI5 flexibility to the application router:

 

modules: requires: - name: demoappdemoapp-ui5-flexibility-keyuser

 

 ​My SmartVariantManagement in View1.view.xml:

 

<svm:SmartVariantManagement id="svm" persistencyKey="BillingFilterBarVariant" showExecuteOnSelection="true" useFavorites="true" />

 

Can somebody help me with this case?

 

Regards,

Timur

entry.title: Which Integration Scenario Are You In? 3 SAP BTP Guides to Help You Move Forward
entry.content:
As organizations accelerate their cloud transformation journeys, integration remains a critical foundation for connecting systems, processes, and data across landscapes.

Within the SAP BTP Value Map, we have published a series of knowledge-based articles to support you in different integration scenarios — whether you are building new capabilities, modernizing existing landscapes, or planning your migration to the cloud.

Below are three key scenarios you might be facing - and how you can get started.

 

🔹Scenario 1: Building Integration Expertise on SAP BTP

If you are an integration consultant or enterprise architect looking to deepen your expertise in SAP’s integration strategy and technologies…

Or if you want to better understand:

  • Core integration capabilities
  • API Management and Event Integration
  • Integration architecture and best practices

👉You may find this guide highly valuable:
SAP Integration Suite Black Belt Empowerment Guide

This enablement program is designed to help you become a subject matter expert in SAP Integration Suite, providing structured learning content, best practices, and practical insights to support your integration journey.

 

🔹Scenario 2: Migrating from SAP Neo to Multi-Cloud (Cloud Foundry)

If you are currently running integration scenarios in the SAP BTP Neo environment and are considering your move to the multi-cloud foundation…

Or if you are asking:

  • What is the right path forward from Neo?
  • How do I migrate my existing integration content?
  • What changes should I expect in Cloud Foundry?

👉This guide walks you through the process step by step:
SAP Neo, Cloud Integration to SAP Integration Suite Migration Guide

It provides practical insights, screenshots, and structured guidance to help you transition your integration scenarios from Neo to SAP Integration Suite on Cloud Foundry.

 

🔹Scenario 3: Transitioning from SAP PI/PO to SAP Integration Suite

If your organization is planning to move away from SAP Process Integration / Process Orchestration (PI/PO)…

Or if you are exploring:

  • Why SAP Integration Suite is the strategic direction
  • How to approach the migration
  • What support services are available

👉You can refer to this article:
How to Migrate from SAP Process Orchestration to SAP Integration Suite through Support Services

This guide outlines the migration approach based on SAP Activate methodology, helping you understand key phases, available options, and how SAP services can support your transition.

 

🚀Start Your Integration Journey with Confidence

Whether you are building new integration capabilities or modernizing your existing landscape, the SAP BTP Value Map provides structured guidance, curated content, and expert insights to support you every step of the way.

👉Explore these articles in the SAP BTP Value Map and take the next step in your integration journey.

We look forward to your feedback and engagement in the community.

entry.title: AI Comes to the Classroom: Updated Academic Lesson on SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
A Lesson That Keeps Pace with the Technology

If you have used the Academic Lesson on "Designing Stories with SAP Analytics Cloud" before, you already know it as a solid bridge between textbook concepts and real-world analytics tooling. The lesson walks students through data exploration in SAP Analytics Cloud, then into the design of impactful data stories, all with free access to SAP Learning Hub, student edition courses and practice systems.

The latest release, **24-sac-001d**, builds on that foundation with a meaningful addition: Activity 3: Applying AI-Powered Visualizations and Augmented Analytics to Business Data in SAP Analytics Cloud.

What Is New in This Update
A Third Activity Focused on AI and Augmented Analytics

The previous version (24-sac-002c, August 2025) included two activities:

Activity 1 - Exploring SAP Analytics Cloud (navigation, modeling, planning features)
Activity 2 - Designing Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud (building and presenting data-driven Stories)

This update adds:

Activity 3 - Applying AI-Powered Visualizations and Augmented Analytics to Business Data in SAP Analytics Cloud

Activity 3 draws from the SAP Learning Hub, student edition course of the same name and introduces students to two units of content:

1. Introducing AI, Machine Learning, and Augmented Analytics - foundational concepts on how AI and machine learning underpin modern business intelligence.
2. Using SAP Analytics Cloud Smart Features - hands-on work with Just Ask, the natural-language query feature that lets users type a question and receive an auto-generated visualization, and Smart Insights, which surfaces data patterns automatically.

Students complete the two units (approximately 52 minutes of course content), earn passing scores on the associated quizzes, and work through a practice exercise where they build a Just Ask Canvas Story live in SAP Analytics Cloud.

New Discussion Questions That Go Beyond the Tool

Activity 3 also comes with three discussion questions designed to engage student critical thinking:

  1. Responsible use of AI-generated visualizations: Students consider what happens when a manager asks them to drop a Just Ask chart into a quarterly business review. Who is responsible for accuracy? What should be verified before presenting AI-generated output?
  2. Data quality and forecasting: Students examine how flawed input data affects forecast reliability, connecting back to the "Garbage In, Garbage Out" principle from Activity 2.
  3. Audience-driven design with AI content: Students compare how a 12-month sales forecast story should be designed differently for an internal sales team versus executive leadership, including how to handle confidence intervals and the visual distinction between actuals and predictions.

These questions are ready to use as class discussions, group activities, essay prompts, or inter-teaching exercises. Suggested answers are included for instructors as a starting point of discussion.

Why This Update Matters for Your Classroom

The shift from specialist-driven analytics to business-user-driven analytics is one of the most significant trends in the data landscape. Organizations increasingly expect graduates, regardless of discipline, to be comfortable interpreting data and questioning AI-generated outputs. 

Here is what makes it practical for teaching

Low barrier to entry - Activity 3 requires only about 52 minutes of course content and one practice exercise. You can add it to an existing assignment without overhauling your syllabus.

No prerequisites beyond the lesson itself -  Students who complete Activities 1 and 2 have all the context they need. The lesson remains accessible to undergraduate and graduate students across business, information systems, social sciences, natural sciences, engineering, medicine, journalism, and beyond.

Free access - The SAP Learning Hub student edition, the courses, and the practice systems are all available at no cost to students and lecturers at institutions of higher education.

Real AI, not simulated -  Students interact with actual AI-powered features in SAP Analytics Cloud. They type natural-language questions, receive generated charts, and evaluate the results, it is hands-on experience with enterprise-grade augmented analytics.

Critical thinking built in - The discussion questions encourage students to treat AI as a starting point, not a finished product. That mindset, evaluating, refining, and contextualizing AI output is one of the most important skill students can develop right now.

How to Get Started

The updated lesson is available for download in two formats:

PDF Version: https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/u6XbMD1?rc=10&doi=SAP1200446](https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/u6XbMD1?rc=10&doi=SAP1200446
Word Version: https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/keWdTC4?rc=10&doi=SAP1191525](https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/keWdTC4?rc=10&doi=SAP1191525

The Word version is fully editable, so you can adapt the student instructions, deliverables, and discussion questions to fit your course design. Add requirements, remove what does not apply, and make it your own.

To access the courses and practice systems, students and lecturers will need an SAP Universal ID and enrollment in the SAP Learning Hub, student edition. If this is your first time using materials as part of the SAP University Alliances program, visit the Getting Started resources linked within the lesson document for setup guidance.

A Note on Flexibility

One of the strengths of this Academic Lesson has always been its adaptability. You do not have to assign all three Activities. You can assign any combination that fits your learning outcomes. You can use the discussion questions as graded essays, informal class discussions, or skip them entirely.

That said, if you are looking for one update to bring into your next offering, Activity 3 is a strong candidate. Giving students hands-on time with AI-augmented analytics, alongside the critical thinking prompts to evaluate what the AI produces, is a combination that serves them well regardless of where their careers take them.

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud TRM-Special G/L indicator not define...
entry.content:
Good day @espani 

Thank you for your question.

In Public Cloud, you do not resolve what you described by changing posting keys with OB41 or similar classic config. Instead, the correction point is the TRM account determination setup. KBA 3719928 explains that scenario.

That error means that the posting generated by the TRM reversal is trying to use a posting key plus account type plus G/L indicator combination that is not valid. As mentioned in KBA 3719928, you must ensure the special G/L indicator used in the TRM posting specification is valid for the account type assigned to the posting key. Also, verify that account symbols such as V.0 are assigned only to vendors, and C.0 only to customers.

So, configuration inconsistencies in TRM account determination are the root cause and not usually the loan reversal app itself. These could be:

  • The posting specification uses a special G/L indicator that does not fit the posting key
  • The posting specification points to the wrong account symbol/account type
  • The same posting specification is reused across scenarios where it should be split, for example paying versus non paying valuation areas.

Your comparison with on premise is understandable, the Public Cloud supported path is not for you to change the posting key definition. The KBA mentioned earlier points to the configuration content, not to OB41.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e5ec5859d8e54df98492d80564a734c0/8e0dda531198434de10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

 

The next step is for you to reproduce the reversal in Process Business Transactions,

identify the affected update type and posting specification and then review whether the posting spec is incorrectly using a customer or vendor special G/L setup, or whether the same posting spec is incorrectly shared across paying and non-paying valuation area scenarios.

 

To summarise:

  • No, in Public Cloud you should not solve your scenario by changing posting key customizing like OB41
  • Yes, the issue is most likely a TRM account determination/Posting specification case
  • The correct place for you to fix the issue is SSCUI 103014.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: 客户项目首付款开票流程
entry.content:
本博文简单介绍Professional Service客户项目首付款开票流程

系统演示

1.创建客户项目，建立开票，案例用的是固定价格合同开票类型

合同总金额3000，首付款1000

2.APP：管理项目开票，点击黄色三角形查看预付款金额

点击设置按钮，把字段Down Payment Received和Down Payment Requested勾选。

选择开票项，点击Prepayment

点击Submit

开票请求已生成

APP:创建开票凭证，设置开票类型FAZ，对首付款进行开票并过账生成日记账

点击Entry View查看分录

3. APP: Post Incoming Payment

查看日记账分录

4.创建总发票，APP:管理项目开票，点击Prepare Billing

开票凭证请求已生成

APP:创建开票凭证，设置开票类型CI01

查看日记账分录，冲销了首付款的分录

感谢观看

entry.title: Re: How to Segragate Payment terms for two different Comp...
entry.content:
Dear @LALIT_RAH1,

In Public Cloud is no standard option to segregate or hide Terms of Payment by company code in the value help. Payment terms are maintained centrally in SSCUI 102934 Maintain Terms of Payment and SAP confirms that related defaults are driven by the payment term itself, not by company code

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2621708

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3207431

The supported approach is to:

  • create separate payment term keys for each company code with clear naming, and
  • default the correct payment term from the relevant master data or document context
  • Feature request to SAP

More details below:

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/11

Summary:

You can separate the payment term codes but not the value-help list by company code in standard Public Cloud. The practical workaround is controlled naming plus correct defaulting from BP/master data

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Unable to complete sap FI certification due to technical errors
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I was curious if other people are having this problem or it is just me.

I have already filed a case: 5000329521

I was able to do previous attempts and it was fine, but now trying to do another attempt, I have had 2 weeks of problems.

First, it started with booking an exam, showing up during the exam time and the exam page not opening up at all. I knew something was wrong before because it wouldn't have the usual "You can start your exam on xxxx date on xxxx time" page. This happened for a week an a half. Even after the exam page wouldn't open, there would be an error trying to book a new one. After emailing SAP support and being told the clear your cache and restart your computer message, I was finally told that there is a deployment issue on the back end and it is now fixed. 

Finally, I booked a time and sat down to do the exam. The exam page opened but the practical system didnt deploy. Great, I am being told there is another deployment issue on the back end. I schedule a new exam and they cancel it without notifying me (which is weird that there isnt an email notifying that your exam time was cancelled). It was cancelled even though things were supposedly fixed, and I've already moved my day around for the exam.

Again, finally, I book the exam, the exam page launches and the practical system launches! Yay! except for the fact that fiori is not working, at all, even after 30 mins of doing as much as I can on GUI. Basically all the questions call for specific fiori apps to be used and I cant access it, meaning I can't complete any question. and yes, I cleared my cahce, restarted my computer, tried a different browser. 

I am writing this to express my frustration because one: as I am at the mercy of the SAP exam schedule, I have to plan my days around when I want to take the exam. It truly sucks to plan my week, expecting to do the exam, get an error and now suddenly because I can't get a response for another few business days, I have to restructure my whole life to do this exam.

Also, why isnt there live supprot for such a new system? Especially one that requires you to schedule ahead? Im not an expert but if i have a problem of being let into an exam, it would be great to be able to get live support, get access to the system within 10 mins and complete the exam in my planned time. I can't keep having to constantly rearrange my life around just to get this certification.

entry.title: Re: Replication of ACDOCA, how fast can we go
entry.content:
Hello,

Obviously, it would depend on the number of delta records generated in the system and the optimization of the CDS  view (but here you're using the standard one) but in our context we are using a copy of I_GLACCOUNTLINEITEMRAWDATA which exposes 228 fields (instead of the standard 428) and which is connected to a worlwide S/4 where we are rolling out many entities every year (utilities sector).

Our replication flow is set in real-time and the impact on S/4 is very low.

You would have an issue if you'd try to replicate treasury data from table FQM_FLOW for exemple (lots of delta records generated with a very short lifecycle - meaning they're flagged for deletion very quickly).

Regards

entry.title: Re: HTML5 (Custom Fiori) app fails with 500 (token exchan...
entry.content:
@ HI cguttikonda24 ,

Thanks for  checking this.
Yes, CAP is bound to the application XSUAA, and the HTML5 Application Repository uses its own SAP‑managed XSUAA. This setup is identical in DEV, TST, and ACC and is the standard SAP pattern. The application works in DEV and ACC with this configuration.

In TST, however, the failure happens in the managed Launchpad runtime while loading static HTML5 resources, before any CAP backend call is reached. We noticed that unlike another working project, this application does not expose the application XSUAA as an instance‑level destination for the HTML5 runtime context. Although DEV and ACC work without it (likely due to existing runtime context).

entry.title: Re: Schedule overhead accounting jobs - select current pe...
entry.content:
Dear @kezelda,

SAP do not documented variable/place-holder that you can enter in Settlement Period / Posting Period / Fiscal Year for Actual Settlement: Projects (SAP) so the job automatically rolls to the current fiscal period/year. SAP documents dynamic behavior only for job templates that explicitly provide dynamic date / dynamic key date handling for example Dynamic Valuation Key Date or dynamic billing date ranges

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/e3f896e4b0af4d1dbf00420e268221b0.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/11742f55d673bc06e10000000a441470.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c56f622a2edf491b9f1b596b55587009/b710108518854368b59b0d8212db3f6d.html

For Actual Settlement: Projects, SAP own KBA shows these are treated as explicit parameters. In particular, SAP states that although Posting Period is not marked mandatory, it is still required if Posting Date is entered, which confirms the template expects concrete fiscal-period input rather than an automatic current period/year variable

So the supported practical approach is:

  • schedule the job as recurring
  • but maintain the relevant Settlement Period / Posting Period / Fiscal Year values explicitly
  • or create/update the job variant each month

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3656677

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Add Value Help to SAP Fiori Elements App
entry.content: Tried the similar approach but the error remains unchanged. MPC_EXT did add the value help entity, metadata is showing the same. However, the DPC_EXT enhancement is little confusing. On my standard service, DPC_EXT was not redefined. So the enhancement had to go to the end of GET_ENTITYSET from DPC(which will be wiped off when SAP regenerates the project). Even then, I see the data not being loaded. My understanding from the error is the GET call is looking for the GET_ENTITYSET implementation of whatever value help entity I have added. Since this entity is added programmatically and no CRUD methods generated for this, it is failing to pull data. Framework is working correctly but it is probably not a recommended approach.

entry.title: Re: Datasphereにてアルファ変換を実施する
entry.content:
Hello,

There is currently no behavior similar to the S/4 alpha transformation. But We continuously strive to improve and expand our system based on useful feedback, and your suggestion is greatly appreciated. I will make sure your request is forwarded to our development team for consideration. In the meantime, we'll do our best to assist you in achieving your goals with our existing features. If you need any help, please don't hesitate to contact us."
I would ask you to follow our SAP standard process for filing feature requests as documented in the SAP note below. The advantage is that your request will be picked up and worked on by the Product Management expert in the Data Intelligence team:

Here is the Datasphere note explaining the process:
3332382 - How to create an enhancement request for SAP Datasphere?

entry.title: Re: SAP Help needed on below items
entry.content:
Good day @anusha_anand 

Thank you for your question.

Question: Can we have default GL account xxxx1000 for list of users when doing GI (cost center, WBS) to pick automatically?

Answer: No. I would not expect a standard user-based defaulting of the VBR G/L account for goods issue to cost center or WBS. In Public Cloud, account determination is a system mechanism that automatically determines accounts when a document is posted, based on the posting context and account assignment details in the line items. Also, note that Manage Default Account Assignments is for supplier claim process, not a general user-based defaulting mechanism for GI postings.

So, if all your plants use the same valuation grouping logic, the standard design remains configuration driven, not user driven. In other words, the Public Cloud does not let you say “user A gets xxxx1000 automatically for GI to cost center or WBS, while user B gets another account” under the same account determination setup.

Question: Can we add created by in view setting within overdue purchase order items app?

Answer: No. The Public Cloud does not validate that option. Overdue Purchase Order Items serves as an analytical monitoring app for overdue PO delivery dates. Does not provide an extensibility to add “created by” in the view setting. Available are the options on the screen.

 

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Need Clarification on Migration of Technical Catalog...
entry.content:
The Migration of Technical Catalogs toolset (/UI2/MGR_TC transaction) enables the migration of technical catalogs from the deprecated format used by the launchpad designer to the current format in the app manager. After the migration, the default tool for managing technical catalogs changes from the deprecated SAP Fiori launchpad designer to the SAP Fiori launchpad app manager, including its persistence or data storage. One more reason is that, there are no change logs for tile/target mapping when using Fiori Launchpad Designer. After the migration , the FLP App Manager provides change logs of Launchpad App Descriptor Items (LADIs)for each migrated catalog. You can either migrate entire catalogs that are ready for migration, or you can partially migrate catalogs that still have non-migratable apps. In the latter case, only the migratable apps are migrated. The following Help document for your reference as well: https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/a7b390faab1140c087b8926571e942b7/1b366ad7ee51467cabd2141a1828ba1a.html?version=202510.001&locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION

Following steps for speed up /ui2/mgr_tc - Export the migration check report and group issues by type.

- Define deterministic, idempotent fix rules (e.g., copy shared tiles locally, recreate target mappings, map deprecated semantic objects).

- Implement dry‑run + batch commits (100–500 items), extensive logging, and an undo/backup plan. - Run on a sandbox first, re-run /ui2/mgr_tc after each pass, and iterate until clean.

- Safety: use only SAP‑supported APIs/apps, avoid direct DB changes, and keep detailed logs. Following note states about the Business Catalogs versus Technical Catalogs.

2616973 - Fiori Reference of Business Catalogs versus Technical Catalogs

Best regards,

Joe

SAP support

entry.title: Re: Err -M3305-The matrial ** does not exist or is not ac...
entry.content:
Hopefuly helpful for someone in the future:

If you get the same error message (M3 305, M3305) when using the SAP Fiori App F6578 (Mass Change BOM) while changing components of the BOM the error happened to me in the FM BOM_MASS_MAINT in the class CL_BOM_ITEM_MASS_MAINT function UPDATE_ITEMS.

The reason was that in the code there is nothing prepened leading zeros to the materialnumber. So changing component 4711 to 4712 showed the error when entering the component number 4712, when entering 000000000000004712 the process did not raise any errors. We use numeric characters for our material numbers so you have to add the leading zeros in this case.

entry.title: Re: Set 'Calculation of Value for Purchase Account' for P...
entry.content:
Dear @CCCHU,

In Public Cloud Calculation of Value for Purchase Account is maintained for the company code, not separately for the plant. Therefore for Plant 6310 / Company Code 6310 the standard setup is to maintain Company Code 6310 with Calculation of Value for Purchase Account = Receipt Value. The relevant Public Cloud configuration is tied to SSCUI 106961 Activate Purchase Account in Company Code

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/60a09f68f2444ceca31dcac2e7017945/f8f82d2c3b5142f49a388f1cccd0a2c2.html

For planned delivery costs SAP confirms that the posting logic must also be checked in SSCUI 101095 Set Calculation Schema Purchasing. The freight condition must use a different purchase account key than the accrual key otherwise delivery-cost invoice posting can fail and any correction affects new purchase orders only

More details below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003460817

Please also note that Purchase Account Management is country-dependent. SAP states that as of release 2508, activation is available only for specific countries if company code 6310 is not in a supported country this setting is not available in standard Public Cloud

More details below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003590597

Summary:

This is not a separate plant-level setting in standard Public Cloud and if planned delivery costs are relevant, also validate SSCUI 101095

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: BAdi Enhancement Implementation using Developer Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Introduction

In On Stack extensibility, Enhancements can be created using BAdi Enhancement Implementation in Developer Extensibility.

Developer extensibility using SAP Cloud ABAP Environment for developer extensions.

 

Business Accelerator Hub

SAP Business Accelerator Hub provides a section for On Stack extensibility (key user extensibility and developer extensibility), to know list of SAP CDS views, APIs, Business Add-ins etc. are available with the release status.

On Stack Extensibility -> Business Add-ins

Search for required BAdi.

This BAdi is related to check purchase order before saving to create it.

Here it shows BAdi Definition and Enhancement spot.

BAdi can be used in both Key User and Developer Extensibility as both states of BAdi is in released state.

Methods and its Parameters

Developer Extensibility – BAdi Enhancement Implementation

Steps to Create Impelementation

Create New BAdi Enhancement Implementation

Chose BAdi Enhancement Implementation.

Add Name and description.

Add Enhancement Spot

BAdi Enhancement Implementation is created.

Choose BAdi Implementation to add Implementations.

Add BAdi Definition and Custom Class name

Add Implementation Class

Choose Implementation Class, add Custom Class name and its description

Implementation Class is created.

 

ZCL_CHECK_PO▸
CLASS zcl_check_po DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
   
   INTERFACES if_badi_interface
   INTERFACES if_ex_mmpur_final_check_po .

  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_check_po IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD if_ex_mmpur_final_check_po~check. 
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

Implementation Class is added to Badi Implementation details

Logic can be added to implementation class

 

ZCL_CHECK_PO▸
CLASS zcl_check_po DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
   
   INTERFACES if_badi_interface
   INTERFACES if_ex_mmpur_final_check_po .

  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_check_po IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD if_ex_mmpur_final_check_po~check. 
    IF purchaseorder-supplier EQ '0000100005'. 
      APPEND VALUE #( messagetype = 'E' messagevariable1= |Choose another Supplier] ) TO messages.
    ENDIF.  
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

Navigate to Create Purchase Order (ME21N)

Provide the required details and check

Error appeared as per logic written.

Add breakpoint in the implementation class to debug.

Place cursor on the logic, choose right click.

Break point is added

Break point can be added in the logic, and it triggers when creating purchase order in the Create Purchase Order (ME21N).

Conclusion

In Developer Extensibility, BAdi Enhancement Implementation can be created and deleted.

Custom Implementation can be captured in TR's and those can be moved to test, production systems.

This custom implementation can also be debugged.

References

  • Extensibility | SAP Help Portal
  • Developer Extensibility | SAP Help Portal
  • Implementing BAdI Enhancement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Business Partner General Data Authorization
entry.content:
Introduction:

In SAP, a Business Partner (BP) represents an individual or organization involved in business processes, and it is used to manage master data such as customers and vendors. Business partner general data like name and address are maintained in one place and then can be extended into roles like customer or vendor based on business needs.

To control the access to this general data, SAP provides authorization objects such as B_BUPA_FDG, B_BUPA_RLT, B_BUPA_ATT, and B_BUPA_GRP. However, understanding how this work in real business scenarios can be confusing.

In this blog, we will explore these authorization objects in a practical way using simple examples.

 

Basic Information on Authorization object in SAP

Authorization Objects are used to restrict the access given to a user.

The major components of authorization concept are as follows,

Authorization object class: It is a container, which encapsulates a number of authorization objects. It has no functional importance except for the encapsulation purpose that helps in easier administration of authorization objects. They are created in transaction su21. The authorizations class AAAB contains authorization objects for Business Partners.

SU21 Transaction

Authorization objects: They are at the heart of the authorization concept. They are the objects that implement actual authorization. They can be looked upon as function modules that import certain values and return a SY-SUBRC value which tells whether a user is authorized or not to access a particular value or not. Authorization objects can be created with the transaction SU21. They have the following structure,

  1. Object: Name of the object
  2. Class: The name of the class to which the above object belongs
  3. Fields: It can accommodate a maximum of 10 fields. These fields along with their data-element have to be created in transaction SU20. This transaction also takes the table name for the data-element entered which will be used to provide F4 help. The values then can be assigned to these fields in PFCG can be set here using the push button PERMITTED VALUES.

In the context of Business Partners has 4 main Authorization Objects provided under standard release.

  • B_BUPA_FDG
  • B_BUPA_RLT
  • B_BUPA_ATT
  • B_BUPA_GRP

Generally, the administrator creates a role to grant the required authorizations to users.

Login with Admin user,
Create a single role in PFCG to manage Business Partner (BP) authorization.
PFCG (Profile Generator) is an SAP transaction used to create roles that control user authorizations in the system.

After creating, add BP transaction in MENU tab as shown below.

In Authorization tab, click on ‘Propose profile name’ to Generate Profile name and Text.

Then click on ‘Change Authorization Data’.
Since we have Assigned BP transaction, the system will generate Authorization objects related to BP automatically, no need to add manually.

Save and generate.

Create a Test User in SU01: ZUSER_TEST.
Note: Creating a test user ensures that access behaves as expected. 

Then in the User tab in PFCG, add the created user and click on User Comparison.

Click on Full Comparison.

Login with the Test user and open the BP transaction (Since we have assigned the transaction, user will have the access to open BP transaction).

Open the BP transaction.

We can observe that the create buttons are disabled, test user doesn’t have access to create BPs.
This is because every Business partner will be created with the general role initially and the user doesn’t have access to the general role.

We can assign the General role to the test user under B_BUPA_RLT role.

 

B_BUPA_RLT:

Exclusively used for managing access to roles. In the authorization role for a particular user, the names of the BP roles for which he should have access to are entered. When this user runs BP transaction, he sees only these roles for which he has authorization.

In this section, we will see how to maintain the B_BUPA_RLT authorization object.

Add general role (000000) in PFCG and provide ACTVT under B_BUPA_RLT.

Note: 

  • 01 for create,
  • 02 for change,
  • 03 for Display,
  • 04 for Display in Value Help,
  • 06 for Delete,

Save and generate.

Now Create buttons are enabled, click on create and fill mandatory fields and save.
Business partners will be created in general role by default.

Now Let’s say we need to provide access to extend the business partner to MKK (Contract partner) role. 
We can choose the MKK role from the dropdown to extend.

Only the roles assigned to a user are visible in the dropdown. Therefore, to ensure the required access, the MKK role must be assigned to the user.

(With Admin user) Add MKK role in PFCG under B_BUPA_RLT.

So now the test user will have the access to create MKK.

Save.

Let’s say we want to restrict that user from changing the Business partner with MKK role, and we need full access to General role.
Click on Manually and add B_BUPA_RLT.

Maintain MKK role and provide create authorization.

Now MKK will appear in the dropdown.

Select MKK role and try to change.

An error message is raised Since no Authorization to change.

 

B_BUPA_FDG

Consider a scenario where certain users should not have the access to change bank details in the BP transaction; this can be achieved using the B_BUPA_FDG authorization object.

Currently user can edit the Bank details.

To restrict the user from changing the Bank details.

Open BUCN transaction and Field-group of Bank details.

Or SPRO → Cross-Application Components → SAP Business Partner → Business Partner → Basic Settings → Authorization Management → Define Field Groups Relevant to Authorizations.

To get Field-group of Bank details, Open BP transaction and use command BDT_ANALYZER.

After that Go to PFCG and restrict change access to B_BUPA_FDG.

Save and generate.

Open the BP in change mode with test user.

‘Bank details’ is non-editable.

 

 B_BUPA_ATT

Consider a scenario where a user is permitted to create Business Partners only when the country is Germany; this can be achieved using the B_BUPA_ATT authorization object.

To achieve this, In SPRO → Cross-Application Components → SAP Business Partner → Business Partner → Basic Settings → Authorization Management → Maintain Authorization Types.

Create an Authorization type,

Take screen field name of country from BP transaction.

Then in PFCG, Provide Auth type and Values and ACTVT.

Open BP with Test user and create BP with country DE.

BP is created, now try to create BP with different value for country.


An error message because the country is IN.

 

B_BUPA_GRP

This object is used to group the BPs so that only a selected number of users can access these BPs. The value for the group is entered when a BP is created. The field is present in the tab ‘control’.

Consider a scenario where Business Partners are assigned to different authorization groups such as GRP1 and GRP2, and a user should be able to create, change, and display Business Partners in GRP1 while having display-only access to GRP2; this can be achieved using the B_BUPA_GRP.

Create 2 Authorization groups GRP1 and GRP2.

To achieve this, In SPRO → Cross-Application Components → SAP Business Partner → Business Partner → Basic Settings → Authorization Management → Maintain Authorization Groups for Business Partner.

Create 2 BPs to demonstrate,

Give Authorization group GRP1 for one BP in control tab.

And GRP2 for another.

Now In PFCG, Give access to GRP1 and GRP2.

Display access to BPs which comes under group GRP1 and all 3 access to GRP2.
So now the test user will only be able to display GRP1 BPs and able to create, change and display the GRP2 BPs.

 Open the 12093 BP which has GRP1 (Display only access).

Change button is disabled for the test user.

If the user tries to create BP with Authorization group GRP1, an error message will be raised.


Open the 12094 BP which has GRP1 (Create, change and display access).


Test user can change the BP and can create.

 

Conclusion:

In conclusion, B_BUPA_RLT, B_BUPA_FDG, B_BUPA_GRP, and B_BUPA_ATT these authorization objects help to control the access to different aspects of Business Partner central data in SAP. Using these objects ensures that users will have access only for what they are allowed to, improving system control and data security.

entry.title: Re: How to change message status from RETRY to FAILED whe...
entry.content:
Hi Himani,

When you use the AMQP Sender adapter, CPI acts as an AMQP consumer, and message lifecycle ownership belongs to the broker (Event Mesh), not CPI.

The FAILED status in CPI Monitoring is only set when:

  • CPI owns the retry lifecycle (e.g., HTTP, SOAP)
  • Or the adapter does not retry at all
For AMQP Sender, retries = broker-managed lifecycle → RETRY forever

entry.title: Re: Creation of Alternative GL Accounts in YACN chart of...
entry.content:
Good day @Claudia234 

Thank you for your question.

As a short answer, yes, the Public Cloud permits you to create additional G/L accounts in a local or alternative chart of accounts. This, however, requires you to assign the alternative chart to the company code and you have the required authorisation.

So, for your case:

  • You are not limited to choosing only the standard alternative accounts already delivered in YACN
  • If the delivered local chart does not fully meet your reporting needs, you have the possibility to extend it with additional local G/L accounts
  • Then link those new YACN accounts to the newly created YCOA accounts as the alternative account number.

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/cb9b75f01584475fa04952c11a67f9fc.html?locale=en-US

3405359 - Local Chart of Accounts in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

3625940 - Unable to change the Alternative Chart of Account Assignment for Company Code - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

See the Blog for more insights: Chart of Accounts in S/4 HANA Public Cloud (Cloud ERP)

You can use Manage Operating Chart of Accounts app (“as an expert”) to create single G/L accounts by copying existing ones. You need the required business catalogs and authorizations to create or change G/L accounts.

To directly answer your question

Question 1: Could you please confirm whether it is permitted in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to create new G/L accounts in the local chart of accounts YACN?

Answer: Yes, in principle it is permitted in Public Cloud, as long as:

  • YACN is assigned as the alternative chart of accounts to the relevant company code.
  • The user has the required authorization for chart of accounts maintenance
  • You create the local account through the supported chart of accounts maintenance process.

Question 2: Alternatively, should we select and assign from the standard alternative G/L accounts already available in YACN?

Answer: Not necessarily. If the required local account does not exist in YACN, the standard Public Cloud approach is for you to create the additional local G/L account in the alternative chart and then assign it to the operating chart account as the alternative account number.

The save option being inactive is likely because the user (you) does not have the required business catalogs or authorization to create or change G/L accounts, you are not in the correct maintenance flow for the alternative chart, and the alternative chart may not be properly assigned to the relevant company code.

See the following reference for more details.

 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/87e8b621685f4f8591078b9dc8840b78.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: RAP Validation Extension in SAP – Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
Introduction 

In the SAP RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming Model), extensibility is a powerful concept that allows developers to enhance standard Business Objects (BOs) without modifying the original code. 

One such enhancement is the Validation Extension, which enables you to add custom validation logic during the save process. 

This is especially useful when: 

  • You do not own the standard Business Object 
  • You want to enforce additional business rules 
  • You must avoid modifying the base implementation 

Scenario 

Assume you are working with a standard RAP Business Object, and you want to validate a field: 

The “Name” field should not contain numeric values 

Even though you don’t own the base BO, you can still implement this validation using RAP Validation Extension. 

RAP Extensibility Overview 

RAP extensibility involves two main roles: 

  1. Extensibility Enabler 
  • The original developer of the BO 
  • Enables the BO for extension 
  1. Extensibility Provider 
  • The developer who creates extensions 
  • Adds custom logic like: 
  • Fields (Data Model Extension) 
  • Validations / Determinations (Behavior Extension) 
  • Nodes 

Note: Extensibility must be enabled first; otherwise, extensions are not possible. 

Step 1: Extensibility Enabler (Base BO Preparation) 

  1. Enable Extensibility in Base Behavior Definition 
managed implementation in class zbp_nvt_i_act1 unique;
strict ( 2 );
extensible
{
    with additional save;
    with determinations on modify;
    with determinations on save;
    with validations on save;
}
  • Enable Node-Level Extensibility
define behavior for znvt_i_act1 //alias <alias_name>
persistent table znvt_dt_act1
lock master
authorization master ( instance )
extensible
{
  create;
  update;
  delete;
  • Maintain Field Mapping (Extensible) 
  mapping for znvt_dt_act1{
  Name = name;
  Orderid = orderid;
  Quantity = quantity;
  Status = zzstatus;
  }
}

Note: If new fields have the same name in CDS and DB table, no mapping extension is required.

  • Enable Projection Behavior Extensibility 
projection;
strict ( 2 );
extensible;

define behavior for znvt_c_act1 //alias <alias_name>
extensible
{
  use create;
  use update;
  use delete;
}

Step 2: Extensibility Provider (Create Validation Extension) 

1. Create Behavior Definition Extension

  • Go to base BDEF: znvt_i_act1 
  • Right-click → New Behavior Extension 
  • Provide: 
    • Package 
    • Extension Name (e.g., znvt_ext1)
    • Description

2. System-Generated Template, for extending the Base Behavior

extension implementation in class zbp_nvt_ext1 unique;

extend behavior for znvt_i_act1
{
   
}

3. Add the Validation Logic

Inside the extension block, define a validation with: 

  • Trigger: on save 
  • Field: Name 
  • Operation: create 
extension implementation in class zbp_nvt_ext1 unique;

extend behavior for znvt_i_act1
{
    validation val_method on save { field Name; create; }
}

Step 3: Implement Validation Logic 

1. Create Behavior Implementation Class 

  • Use quick fix (Ctrl + 1) on the implementation class 
  • This will generate class: zbp_nvt_ext1 
  • Implement your validation logic inside this class 
CLASS lhc_znvt_i_act1 DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.

    METHODS val_method FOR VALIDATE ON SAVE
      IMPORTING keys FOR znvt_i_act1~val_method.

ENDCLASS.

Implementation: 

CLASS lhc_znvt_i_act1 IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD val_method.
    read entity in local mode znvt_i_act1
    aLL FIELDS WITH corrESPONDING #( keys )
    reSULT data(lt_data).

    data(ls_data) = lt_data[ 1 ].

    if ls_data-Name ca '1234567890'.
    appEND vaLUE #( %tky = ls_data-%tky
                    %element-Name = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                    %msg = new_message_with_text( severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
                    text = 'the Name field can not contain numbers'
                 ) ) to reported-znvt_i_act1.
    endIF.

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

Optional: Projection Behavior Extension 

You can also extend the projection layer if needed. 

However, for validation scenarios, this step is usually not required. 

Output 

  • When a user enters numeric values in the "Name" field 
  • And tries to save the data 

The system throws an error message: "The Name field cannot contain numbers" .

Conclusion 

RAP Validation Extensions provide a clean and upgrade-safe way to implement custom validations without modifying the original Business Object. 

By properly using: 

  • Extensibility Enabler 
  • Behavior Extensions 
  • Validation Methods

entry.title: Triggering Background Jobs from RAP Actions – A Practical Guide
entry.content:
Introduction:


In the SAP ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP), actions are typically executed within a single transactional request. However, resource-intensive operations are not suitable for synchronous execution as they impact performance and user experience. To handle this, background job processing can be used to execute heavy logic asynchronously. This blog demonstrates how a RAP action can trigger a background job to keep the main transaction lightweight.

What is a RAP Action?
An action in RAP is a non-standard operation used to change data or trigger specific business logic. By default, these are synchronous, meaning the UI waits for the logic to finish before the user can continue.

What is Background Processing (BG)?
Background processing allows the system to run tasks in a separate session without user interaction. It is ideal for "fire-and-forget" scenarios or resource-intensive tasks that would otherwise cause a timeout in a web session.

Scenario: Bulk Asynchronous Processing in RAP
A business user needs to process multiple records simultaneously in a SAP Fiori application—such as updating statuses for a large batch of background job entries. While a simple status change is quick, the underlying business logic (like complex data validation or external system integration) is resource-intensive and long-running.

Problem Statement
Executing heavy synchronous logic directly within RAP actions causes poor user experience, performance degradation, and potential transaction timeouts.

Solution
Trigger an asynchronous background job from the RAP action. The action marks the records as "In Progress," and the Saver Class calls a Function Module IN BACKGROUND TASK to schedule a background report. This offloads the processing to a separate session, allowing the main UI transaction to remain lightweight and responsive.

 

Implementation Steps

Step 1: Create a database table

@EndUserText.label : 'DATABASE TABLE FOR BACKGROUND JOB' 
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE 
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT 
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A 
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED 

define table zaf_dt_backjob { 

key id : uuid not null; 
description : abap.char(50); 
status : abap.char(10); 
job_name : abap.char(32); 
job_count : abap.char(10); 
start_time : timestampl; 
end_time : timestampl; 
error_message : abap.char(255); 
created_by : abap.char(12);
created_at : abp_creation_tstmpl; 
last_changed_at : abp_lastchange_tstmpl; 

}

Step 2: Create a interface view on top of database table.

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE] 
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED 
@EndUserText.label: 'BACKGROUND job interface view' 
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true 

define root view entity zi_AF_backjob as select from zaf_dt_backjob 

{ 
key id as Id, 
description as Description, 
status as Status, 
job_name as JobName, 
job_count as JobCount, 
start_time as StartTime, 
end_time as EndTime, 
error_message as ErrorMessage, 
created_by as CreatedBy, 
created_at as CreatedAt, 
last_changed_at as LastChangedAt, 
case status 
when 'DONE' then 3 
when 'INPR' then 2 
when 'NEW' then 1 
when 'ERR' then 4 
else 0 
end as StatusCriticality 

}

 

 Step 3: Create a Consumption view

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Background job projection view'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@UI.headerInfo: {
  typeName: 'Background Job',
  typeNamePlural: 'Background Jobs',
  title: { type: #STANDARD, value: 'Description' }
}
define root view entity zc_af_backjob
provider contract transactional_query
as projection on zi_AF_backjob
{

key Id,
  Description,
  Status,
  JobName,
  JobCount,
  StartTime,
  EndTime,
  ErrorMessage,
  CreatedAt,
  LastChangedAt,
  StatusCriticality

}

Step 4: Metadata Extension file 

@Metadata.layer: #CORE
annotate entity zc_af_backjob with 
{
  .facet: [
    {
      id: 'General',
      purpose: #STANDARD,
      type: #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE,
      label: 'General Information',
      position: 10
    }
  ]

  .lineItem: [{ position: 10, label: 'UUID' }]
  .identification: [{ position: 10, label: 'UUID' }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 10 }]
  Id;

  .lineItem: [{ position: 20, label: 'Description' }]
  .identification: [{ position: 20, label: 'Description' }]
  Description;

  .lineItem: [
    { position: 30, label: 'Status' },
    { type: #FOR_ACTION, dataAction: 'StartProcessing', label: 'Start Job' }
  ]
  .identification: [{ position: 30, label: 'Status' }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 30 }]
  .dataPoint: {
    title: 'Status',
    criticality: 'StatusCriticality'
  }
  Status;

  .lineItem: [{ position: 40, label: 'Job Name' }]
  .identification: [{ position: 40, label: 'Job Name' }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 40 }]
  JobName;

  .lineItem: [{ position: 50, label: 'Job Count' }]
  .identification: [{ position: 50, label: 'Job Count' }]
  JobCount;

  .lineItem: [{ position: 60, label: 'Start Time' }]
  .identification: [{ position: 60, label: 'Start Time' }]
  StartTime;

  .lineItem: [{ position: 70, label: 'End Time' }]
  .identification: [{ position: 70, label: 'End Time' }]
  EndTime;

  .lineItem: [{ position: 80, label: 'Error Message' }]
  .identification: [{ position: 80, label: 'Error Message' }]
  ErrorMessage;

  .hidden: true
  CreatedAt;

  .hidden: true
  LastChangedAt;

  .hidden: true
  StatusCriticality;
}

Step 5:  Create a Behavior Definition on top of interface View

unmanaged implementation in class zbp_i_af_backjob unique;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;

define behavior for zi_AF_backjob 
lock master
total etag LastChangedAt
draft table zaf_draft_bg
authorization master ( instance )
early numbering

{
  create;
  update;
  delete;

     draft action activate optimized;
     draft action resume;
     draft action Edit;
     draft action Discard;
     draft determine action Prepare;

    action StartProcessing result [1] $self;

  determination SetInitialStatus on modify { create; }

 field ( readonly ) Id;
  field ( readonly ) Status, JobName, JobCount, StartTime, EndTime, ErrorMessage;
  field ( readonly ) CreatedAt, LastChangedAt, CreatedBy;

  field ( mandatory ) Description;


mapping for zaf_dt_backjob{

CreatedAt = created_at;
CreatedBy = created_by;
Description = description;
EndTime = end_time;
ErrorMessage = error_message;
Id = id;
JobCount = job_count;
JobName = job_name;
LastChangedAt = last_changed_at;
StartTime = start_time;
Status = status;

}
}

Step 6: Create implementation class

CLASS lhc_zi_af_backjob DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    TYPES: tt_backjob_db TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zaf_dt_backjob WITH EMPTY KEY.

    " Buffer for CRUD and Job Scheduling
    CLASS-DATA: mt_buffer_upsert TYPE tt_backjob_db,
                mt_buffer_delete TYPE tt_backjob_db,
                gt_jobs          TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zaf_dt_backjob-id.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS get_instance_authorizations FOR INSTANCE AUTHORIZATION
      IMPORTING keys REQUEST requested_authorizations FOR zi_af_backjob RESULT result.

    METHODS earlynumbering_create FOR NUMBERING
      IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE zi_af_backjob.

    METHODS create FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE zi_af_backjob.

    METHODS update FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING entities FOR UPDATE zi_af_backjob.

    METHODS delete FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING keys FOR DELETE zi_af_backjob.

    METHODS read FOR READ
      IMPORTING keys FOR READ zi_af_backjob RESULT result.

    METHODS lock FOR LOCK
      IMPORTING keys FOR LOCK zi_af_backjob.

    METHODS StartProcessing FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION zi_af_backjob~StartProcessing RESULT result.

    METHODS SetInitialStatus FOR DETERMINE ON MODIFY
      IMPORTING keys FOR zi_af_backjob~SetInitialStatus.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lhc_zi_af_backjob IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD get_instance_authorizations.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD earlynumbering_create.
    LOOP AT entities INTO DATA(ls_entity).
      IF ls_entity-id IS NOT INITIAL.
        CONTINUE.
      ENDIF.

      DATA(lv_uuid) = cl_system_uuid=>create_uuid_x16_static( ).

      APPEND VALUE #(
        %cid      = ls_entity-%cid
        %is_draft = ls_entity-%is_draft
        id        = lv_uuid
      ) TO mapped-zi_af_backjob.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD create.
    LOOP AT entities INTO DATA(ls_entity).
      APPEND VALUE #(
        id          = ls_entity-id
        description = ls_entity-Description
        status      = 'NEW'
        created_by  = sy-uname
      ) TO mt_buffer_upsert.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD update.
    LOOP AT entities INTO DATA(ls_entity).
      " In unmanaged, we must fetch current state to apply partial updates
      SELECT SINGLE * FROM zaf_dt_backjob WHERE id = _entity-id INTO (ls_db).

      IF ls_entity-%control-Description = if_abap_behv=>mk-on.
         ls_db-description = ls_entity-Description.
      ENDIF.

      IF ls_entity-%control-Status = if_abap_behv=>mk-on.
         ls_db-status = ls_entity-Status.
      ENDIF.

      GET TIME STAMP FIELD ls_db-last_changed_at.
      APPEND ls_db TO mt_buffer_upsert.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD delete.
    LOOP AT keys INTO DATA(ls_key).
      APPEND VALUE #( id = ls_key-id ) TO mt_buffer_delete.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD read.
    IF keys IS INITIAL. RETURN. ENDIF.
    SELECT * FROM zaf_dt_backjob FOR ALL ENTRIES IN  WHERE id = -id INTO TABLE (lt_data).

    result = CORRESPONDING #( lt_data MAPPING
      Id = id Description = description Status = status JobName = job_name
      JobCount = job_count StartTime = start_time EndTime = end_time
      ErrorMessage = error_message CreatedBy = created_by CreatedAt = created_at
      LastChangedAt = last_changed_at ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD lock.
    
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD StartProcessing.
    LOOP AT keys INTO DATA(ls_key).
      " 1. Mark status as 'INPR' immediately in the database
      UPDATE zaf_dt_backjob SET status = 'INPR' WHERE id = _key-id.

      " 2. Add to global job queue for the SAVE phase
      INSERT ls_key-id INTO TABLE gt_jobs.
    ENDLOOP.

    result = VALUE #( FOR key IN keys ( Id = key-id %param = CORRESPONDING #( key ) ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD SetInitialStatus.
    LOOP AT keys INTO DATA(ls_key).
      SELECT SINGLE * FROM zaf_dt_backjob WHERE id = _key-id INTO (ls_db).
      IF ls_db-status IS INITIAL.
        ls_db-status = 'NEW'.
        APPEND ls_db TO mt_buffer_upsert.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

" --- Saver Class Implementation ---
CLASS lsc_zi_af_backjob DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_saver.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
    METHODS finalize REDEFINITION.
    METHODS check_before_save REDEFINITION.
    METHODS save REDEFINITION.
    METHODS cleanup REDEFINITION.
    METHODS cleanup_finalize REDEFINITION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS lsc_zi_af_backjob IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD finalize.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD check_before_save.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD save.
    " 1. Standard DB Persistence
    IF lhc_zi_af_backjob=>mt_buffer_delete IS NOT INITIAL.
      DELETE zaf_dt_backjob FROM TABLE @lhc_zi_af_backjob=>mt_buffer_delete.
    ENDIF.

    IF lhc_zi_af_backjob=>mt_buffer_upsert IS NOT INITIAL.
      MODIFY zaf_dt_backjob FROM TABLE @lhc_zi_af_backjob=>mt_buffer_upsert.
    ENDIF.

    " 2. Trigger Async logic using the reference pattern
    LOOP AT lhc_zi_af_backjob=>gt_jobs INTO DATA(lv_id).
      CALL FUNCTION 'ZAF_FM_BACKJOB'
        IN BACKGROUND TASK
        DESTINATION 'NONE'  " Critical for separate LUW execution
        EXPORTING
          iv_id = lv_id.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD cleanup.
    " Clear all buffers after the LUW is complete
    CLEAR: lhc_zi_af_backjob=>mt_buffer_upsert,
           lhc_zi_af_backjob=>mt_buffer_delete,
           lhc_zi_af_backjob=>gt_jobs.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD cleanup_finalize.
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

Step 7: Create behavior definition on Consumption view

projection;
strict ( 2 );
use draft;

define behavior for zc_af_backjob //alias <alias_name>
{
  use create;
  use update;
  use delete;

  use action StartProcessing;

  use action activate;
  use action resume;
  use action Edit;
  use action Discard;
  use action Prepare;
}

Step 8: Create service Definition on top of Consumption view

@EndUserText.label: 'service defination for background job'
define service Zsevd_bg_job {
  expose zc_af_backjob;
}

Step 9: Create service binding on top of service defination

Step 10: Create Function Module

FUNCTION zaf_fm_backjob.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"  IMPORTING VALUE(IV_ID) TYPE ZAF_DT_BACKJOB-ID
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
  DATA: lv_jobname   TYPE tbtcjob-jobname,
        lv_jobcount  TYPE tbtcjob-jobcount,
        lv_timestamp TYPE timestampl.

  " 1. Dynamic Job Name for better monitoring in SM37
  lv_jobname = |ZAF_BG_{ iv_id+0(8) }|.

  CALL FUNCTION 'JOB_OPEN'
    EXPORTING jobname = lv_jobname
    IMPORTING jobcount = lv_jobcount.

  IF sy-subrc = 0.
    GET TIME STAMP FIELD lv_timestamp.

    " 2. Update DB with system metadata immediately
    UPDATE zaf_dt_backjob
      SET job_name   = _jobname,
          job_count  = _jobcount,
          status     = 'INPR',
          start_time = _timestamp
      WHERE id = _id.

    " 3. Push data to UI so it's visible while report processes
    COMMIT WORK.
  ENDIF.

  " 4. Schedule the report
  SUBMIT zaf_bg_job_report WITH p_id = iv_id
    VIA JOB lv_jobname NUMBER lv_jobcount AND RETURN.

  " 5. Release job
  CALL FUNCTION 'JOB_CLOSE'
    EXPORTING jobname = lv_jobname jobcount = lv_jobcount strtimmed = 'X'.
ENDFUNCTION.

Step 11: Create a Report Program

REPORT zaf_bg_job_report.

PARAMETERS: p_id TYPE zaf_dt_backjob-id.

DATA: lv_timestamp TYPE timestampl.

START-OF-SELECTION.

  IF p_id IS INITIAL.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  WAIT UP TO 5 SECONDS.

  GET TIME STAMP FIELD lv_timestamp.

  " Update final status to match the reference 'DONE'
  UPDATE zaf_dt_backjob
    SET status        = 'DONE',
        end_time      = _timestamp,
        error_message = ''
    WHERE id = @p_id.

  COMMIT WORK.

Result :

Select Multiple Records then click Action Button Start Job.

 

 

Conclusion :

This approach demonstrates how the SAP ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) can be extended beyond standard transactional processing to meet demanding real-world requirements. By leveraging the Saver Class to trigger asynchronous background jobs, you ensure that resource-intensive operations—such as bulk status updates in SD or MM—are executed efficiently without affecting the performance of the main transaction.

entry.title: Bridging the Physical-Digital Divide: Proof of Presence for SAP EAM, TM, and SuccessFactors
entry.content:
In our journey toward the Intelligent Enterprise, data integrity is everything. We rely on SAP as our single source of truth. However, when it comes to remote physical operations – whether a technician inspecting a pipeline in SAP EAM, a field crew logging hours in SuccessFactors, or a logistics provider reaching a warehouse dock in SAP EWM – verifying actual physical presence at a specific time remains a conundrum.

While standard GPS or static barcodes offer basic tracking, they can be manipulated and often lack the mathematical certainty required for rigorous compliance, payroll accuracy, and safety audits. To achieve true "4D compliance" (verifying location and time simultaneously), organizations need an offline-capable solution that seamlessly integrates into the SAP ecosystem.

That is why I developed IAT4D (Identity Attendance Tracking) platform - see a whitepaper. Combined with SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), it provides the foundation to integrate and automate these secure workflows across an enterprise. Check the iFlow code in my GitHub.

The Architecture: Secure, Offline, and Clean Core Compliant

IAT4D utilizes edge-compute identity chips (iMarks) embedded in durable materials for site tagging. These passive markers require no power source or satellite signals, operating entirely on standard Apple CoreNFC and ISO 7816 smart-card technology.

Here is how it integrates with our existing SAP environment to guarantee unspoofable proof of presence:

  1. The Interaction: A field worker uses a standard SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) application to tap their mobile device against the physical iMark at the site.
  2. Dynamic Signature: Instead of broadcasting a static ID, the iMark electronically signs a dynamic, one-time passcode using Ed25519 cryptography. This creates a unique tamper-resistant digital bundle containing the location and the time.
  3. SAP Integration Suite: The mobile app forwards this as a lightweight JSON payload to SAP Cloud Integration (CPI).
  4. Verification: Using a pre-built Groovy script, SAP CPI verifies the cryptographic signature against an individual verification key. If successful, the system of record updates instantly:

This approach aligns perfectly with SAP’s Clean Core strategy. By keeping the cryptographic verification within SAP BTP, we ensure that only clean, mathematically verified data feeds into S/4HANA or SuccessFactors.

Bringing cryptographic proof of presence into standard SAP workflows unlocks immediate operational excellence across several core modules:

SAP EAM / Plant Maintenance (PM) & FSM
For critical assets like remote HVAC systems, lifts, rail switches, or pipelines, compliance is non-negotiable. By affixing an iMark directly to the physical asset, a technician’s tap provides verifiable proof that a safety inspection or maintenance routine actually occurred on-site. This transforms routine maintenance logs into immutable, audit-ready compliance records.

SAP SuccessFactors & SAP Fieldglass
Tracking time and attendance for sparse field workers, remote construction crews, or third-party contractors often happens in environments where networked biometric scanners are impossible to deploy. A rugged, offline iMark across service area allows workers to securely check in using their mobile devices. This ensures absolute payroll accuracy and protects the integrity of HR data.

SAP TM / EWM / Global Track and Trace (GTT)
Supply chain visibility relies on accurate milestone tracking. Tapping an iMark at a loading dock, specific drop-off checkpoint, or secure warehouse zone guarantees that logistics providers have reached the precise physical location. This eliminates the unreliability of fluctuating GPS signals and dramatically strengthens the track and trace capabilities.

How is your organization currently managing attendance verification for remote assets and field teams? Let’s discuss in the comments below...

entry.title: Access/ Unauthorized services in SAP BTP Trial: Fixes
entry.content:
Access Denied/ Unauthorized across SAP BTP Trial Services
SAP Business Application Studio (BAS)

I subscribed to BAS in my trial account and I was ready to open the application, it says
Access Denied – You do not have the necessary roles.

BAS requires role collections like Business_Application_Studio_Developer or Business_Application_Studio_Administrator.
Even though the service was entitled and subscribed, I had no roles assigned.

Go to BTP Cockpit → Subaccount → Entitlements → make sure BAS is listed. otherwise, click Edit  → Add Service Plans → search for BAS as follows. Select the plans based on your requirement.


Subscribe to BAS in Service Marketplace by clicking on Create (choose developer/ required plan).


Go to Security → Users, assign role collection: Select the required roles, go back to market place as shown above and click on Go to Application.
Business_Application_Studio_Developer
Business_Application_Studio_Administrator


Log out and back in if you see any errors. After this, BAS opened instantly. 

SAP Build Apps

I opened SAP Build Apps to build an app. It loaded, but most buttons were disabled, and deployment failed.

Build Apps needs role collections like Build_Apps_Developer or Build_Apps_Administrator.
Without them, you can see the UI, but cannot fully interact.

Check entitlements for SAP Build Apps. Go to BTP Cockpit → Subaccount → Entitlements → Edit → Add Service Plan → Follow below

Subscribe to Build Apps plan in Service Marketplace as done for SAP BAS.
Assign roles under Security → Users:
Build_Apps_Developer
Build_Apps_Administrator
Re-login to apply roles.

For trial accounts, always assign both developer and admin roles to avoid hidden restrictions for all necessary services.

Lessons Learned Across All Services
Trial doesn’t mean preconfigured – almost every service needs entitlements, subscriptions, and roles.
Roles are critical – without them, you may see the service, but can’t use it.
Re-login matters – changes don’t always take effect immediately.

If you follow this sequence across all services, errors become a clue rather than a blocker.

Note: This blog post is originally written based on my personal experience and knowledge. I have ensured that the content reflects my own understanding.
Thank you!!
BR// Dhanasupriya Sidagam

entry.title: Wrong Volume Calculation in Delivery
entry.content: Wrong Volume is getting calculated in Delivery Header in SAP. It is getting calculated correctly at item level. Packing also happening in this case. What could be the reason for wrong Volume Calculation at header level? This is happening only for 1 material and the stacking factor is changed from 1 to 3 in recent changes. Is that the reason? Or Do I need to check any config related to packing? Please advise.

entry.title: Re: 1H 2026 - Release highlights of SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding
entry.content: Nice blog, Thanks Sachine.

entry.title: Re: Can the ATC baseline be considered in regular batch runs for the S4HANA_READINESS_2023 check?
entry.content:
Update: With @OlgaDolinskaja's help I was able to figure out that I had the checkbox to "consider baseline" set, just unfortunately in the wrong but very similarly named run-definition! So, the issue was easy to resolve and the baseline kicked in as expected with the next run.

==> Tip: use more distinct names than xxx_S4HANA_Z_ALL vs. xxx_S4HANA_ALL_Z for the run series to not as easily confuse yourself as happened for me!

Cheers

Bärbel

entry.title: Setting Mandatory Fields in Purchase Requisitions via Custom Logic in Public Cloud
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, Purchase Requisitions (PRs) often require additional field validations to ensure data completeness and process compliance. While some fields can be defined as mandatory through standard customizing, this is limited to predefined fields and specific scenarios.

This blog post explains how to set additional standard fields as mandatory in Purchase Requisitions by using Custom Logic with Key User Extensibility.

The solution applies to the following apps:

  • Manage Purchase Requisitions – Professional
  • Create Purchase Requisition – Advanced

Business Requirement

The business requirement was to enforce mandatory entry of the following fields at purchase requisition item level:

  • Purchasing Organization (Field EKORG)
  • Requisitioner (Field AFNAM)

Why standard customizing is not sufficient

Using the customizing activity Maintain Account Assignment Categories (ID 101815), some fields (for example Cost Center) can be set as mandatory. However:

  • This applies only during the creation of an account assignment category
  • Only predefined fields are available
  • Additional standard fields cannot be enforced

To cover these gaps, Custom Logic (BAdIs) must be used.

Solution Overview

To fulfill the requirements, two different Custom Logic extension points are used:

Both extension points are available via the Custom Logic app in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Custom Logic 1: Change of Field Control (Purchasing Organization)

Extension Point: Change of Field Control for Purchase Requisitions

Extension Point ID: MM_PUR_S4_PR_FLDCNTRL_SIMPLE

Description

This BAdI allows you to:

  • Hide fields
  • Set fields as read-only
  • Set fields as mandatory
  • Set fields as optional

It works directly at UI field control level.

⚠️Limitation
This extension point supports the field PURREQNSSPREQUESTOR, which is relevant only for Self‑Service Procurement.
It does not enforce the Requisitioner field in apps Manage Purchase Requisitions - Professional or Create Purchase Requisition - Advanced.

Sample Code –  Change of Field Control (Purchasing Organization) 

* Sample Code

    FIELD-SYMBOLS <fs> TYPE mmpur_s_pr_fldsel.


   READ TABLE fieldselection_table ASSIGNING <fs> WITH KEY field = 'PURCHASINGORGANIZATION'.
    IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
     <fs>-fieldstatus = '+'.
    ENDIF.

* END OF Sample Code
 
✔️  Result:
  • The Purchasing Organization field becomes mandatory on PR item level.

Custom Logic 2: Check of Purchase Requisition Item (Requisitioner)

Since the Requisitioner (AFNAM) cannot be enforced via field control, a check-based validation is required.

Extension Point: Check of Purchase Requisition Item

Extension Point ID: MM_PUR_S4_PR_CHECK

Description

This BAdI allows you to:

  • Perform custom validations
  • Trigger error messages
  • Prevent saving the Purchase Requisition

Sample Code – Check of Purchase Requisition Item (Requisitioner)

* Sample Code

DATA: ls_message LIKE LINE OF messages.

IF PURCHASEREQUISITION-PURCHASINGDOCUMENTTYPE = 'NB'.
LOOP AT purchaserequisitionitem_table INTO DATA(ls_item).
      IF ls_item-PURREQNREQUESTOR = ''.

         APPEND VALUE #( messagetype = 'E'
         messageid = 'ME'
         messagenumber = '999'
         messagevariable1 = 'Enter Requisitioner / Anforderer fehlt' ) TO messages.
         purchaserequisitionhaserror = abap_true.
         RETURN.
      ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDIF.

* END OF Sample Code

 

✔️  Result:
  • The PR cannot be saved if the Requisitioner is missing
  • A clear error message is displayed to the user

Transport

Custom Logic implementations are not transported automatically and must be added to a Software Collection.

Steps

  1. Open Export Software Collection
  2. Select an existing collection or create a new one
  3. Choose Add Items
  4. Filter by:
    • Last Changed On
    • Last Changed By
  5. Add the Custom Logic entries
  6. Transport the collection as usual

Summary

By combining two Custom Logic extension points, it is possible to:

  • Enforce mandatory fields beyond standard customizing limits
  • Apply validations consistently across professional and advanced PR apps
  • Stay fully within SAP-recommended extensibility

This approach provides a flexible and upgrade-safe way to enhance Purchase Requisition quality.

References

  • 3345421 - BAdI: Field Control for Purchase Requisitions - SAP for Me
  • How to Manage and Export Your Software Collection | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Q1 2026 Quarterly Release Highlights: SAP BTP Security and Identity & Access Management
entry.content:
Another quarter done and dusted! We kicked off the new year with several new features and capabilities.

For a complete overview of feature deliveries for SAP Cloud Identity Services, check out our list of all new feature announcements in the SAP Cloud Identity Services Release Notes on the SAP Help Portal.

SAP Cloud Identity Services: Multiple corporate identity providers with the same issuer

In large enterprise environments, a single corporate identity provider often can't satisfy every trust requirement — especially when different business units have conflicting needs. Based on a customer improvement request, SAP Cloud Identity Services now supports multiple OpenID Connect corporate IdP configurations sharing the same issuer URL, giving teams the flexibility they need. For more information, see SAP Note 3718653 

SAP Cloud Identity Services: Authorization Management Service API for customer-specific policies

The Authorization Management (AMS) component of SAP Cloud Identity Services now offers an API that allows you to maintain customer-specific policies. This is a meaningful step toward automating governance and reducing manual overhead for security teams. For general information on AMS, please check the SAP Cloud Identity Services Developer Guide.  

SAP Custom Domain Service: New REST API for automatic certificate renewal

Managing domain certificates manually is error-prone — and with certificate lifespans soon set to drop to 90 days or less, automation becomes essential. The SAP Custom Domain Service now includes a REST API that handles certificate renewal automatically. It allows customers to build an integration with both on-premise and cloud-based Certificate Lifecycle Management processes. For details, please check the SAP Help Portal.

Stay in the loop

Join the SAP BTP Security and SAP Cloud Identity Services communities to get updates, share feedback, and connect with others!
